note: Maintained log. Updated deliberately when we work a project or when Jack sends a wrap-up. Not auto-inferred; not touched by the hourly job.

projects.name: FADE
projects.headline: Product is launch-ready — held up only by an external D-U-N-S delay.
projects.lastUpdate: This weekend shipped Thijmen's full customization set: Team leagues, a two-step Create wizard with inline explanations, a Reactivate flow, a Past Leagues tab, a cleaner match/prop-only market-cap picker, and public signup (Survivor cut). It's live to test at /signup. The one blocker is outside your control: the D-U-N-S application (#34667209) is 8 business days overdue at D&B, which keeps iOS TestFlight and the App Store locked — so native mobile could slip past the Aug 16 EPL opener.
nextSteps: Wait 24h on the D&B comment before escalating to Apple Developer Support.
Enable email confirmation in Supabase Auth (5-min task; unblocks password reset for invited users).
Kick off the Capacitor Android wrapper — Android is unblocked, so Google Play can ship while iOS waits.
Add content moderation on avatar uploads before public launch.

projects.name: LAFC Analytics
projects.headline: Dashboard rebuilt and live; a full-season data backfill is running now.
projects.lastUpdate: Restored and live on a fresh Supabase project (lafc-analytics): all 16 LAFC matches loaded, RLS locked read-only, and the season-stats view plus starter RPC rebuilt. The scraper is fixed (runs headful to beat WhoScored's anti-bot). Right now it's backfilling the full MLS season — ~207 matches over 6–7 hours, resumable if it stalls — to build the percentile pool the player-fingerprint feature needs.
nextSteps: Confirm the morning summary shows the percentile pool loaded.
Build the fingerprint layer on top — percentile profiles, DNA composites, and the Scout ranker.
Rebuild the match-report Edge Function so the Generate Report button works again.

projects.name: Public portfolio
projects.headline: Live and proven — one cache refresh from fully in sync with your notebook.
projects.lastUpdate: The editorial layout is live: a dark Tzolis hero anchoring a one-screen home, four Substack articles, the Yumpu presentation, three LinkedIn posts, and the heatmap wired into the dossier header. The architecture is proven — the portfolio and your notebook share one database, so a report edited in the terminal flows straight to the public page. Your latest Tzolis edit is already in the database, just waiting on a cache refresh.
nextSteps: Force a redeploy to push the pending Tzolis text edit live; decide whether to shorten the revalidation window so future notebook edits appear within minutes.
Port the scouting terminal's real report layout onto the dossier page so it matches the notebook (still rendering the earlier invented format).

projects.name: ScoutDraft
projects.headline: Open-signup and browser-verified — ready for your test pass.
projects.lastUpdate: A working, open-signup scouting app (public notebook): the full 2,612-player pool from Sportmonks with trustworthy within-position percentiles, plus a stats panel, radar toggle, filtering, Compare, and shortlists — all browser-verified. You deliberately cut the Galaxy feature and parked the Transfermarkt market-value idea (no legal way to source it).
nextSteps: Run the 12-step test pass and send back what to change (becomes the next build prompt).
UX friction cleanup, then a colors pass.
Wire Resend SMTP so password reset works.

projects.name: SCOUTING_TERMINAL
projects.headline: Full suite live; World Cup logging underway, Tzolis nearly complete.
projects.lastUpdate: Live with the full module suite (personal notebook) — Players, Teams, Match Notes, Compare, Tactical Board, Shortlists, and a new STATS tab — in a cream editorial style (Playfair Display, Space Mono, Inter). The World Cup 2026 transcription workflow is active with 14+ matches logged through the Round of 32, and the JB Report PDF generates clean single-page player reports with heatmaps and color-coded cards. Tzolis has a complete onboarding report from 3 all-action watches — heatmap and archetypes in — but his radar scores and Stats API ID are still pending.
nextSteps: Run the em-dash SQL cleanup on Tzolis' DB fields.
Build Tzolis' radar scores from his notes.
Keep logging World Cup matches as you watch them.
Run the queued URL-overflow + STATS-tab + card-text-size prompt.

projects.name: FanSlate
projects.headline: 3,600 grounded blurbs done — one human review from deploy.
projects.lastUpdate: The concept is 'your American teams, your English club': pick across NFL/NBA/MLB/NHL/CFB/CBB and 'Reveal my club' matches you to an English side. You just generated all 3,600 grounded per-team blurbs — cached and passing the automated checks (no em-dashes, no length outliers, no dupes) — replacing the old thin, repetitive copy with specific, fact-grounded lines for every pair. The only gate left is human: a banned-phrase sweep and a voice read of a sample.
nextSteps: Commit the work — 3,600 blurbs and the API spend are uncommitted right now.
Confirm the result page reads from blurbs.json (old template as fallback), then push and deploy.
Then: mobile polish and the socials launch content.
